clauses.detail: 15-year term (new), lesser for transfers, renewal term from expiring for renewals. One renewal for shorter of 15 years or remaining lease term. Expiration date set by Company on cover page.

Written notice 3-6 months before; sign then-current agreement (may differ materially); comply with modernization (Section 5.5); good standing, no substantial noncompliance history; right to premises throughout renewal; pay renewal fee; sign release; renewal location approved.

CLF 5% of Gross Sales; SPF up to 6%; interest 18% or max legal rate; $50 late fee per occurrence. Electronic funds transfer authorization required — Company designates amount to transfer from franchisee account.

Must purchase, install, maintain EPOS and Computer Systems from designated sources. Must pay for all future updates/supplements/modifications. Must allow Company access to all data at all times. Must maintain internet connection and email. No contractual limitation on frequency/cost of upgrades.

Must use only approved products. Company can designate single source for 6 categories. Company can designate single source for ANY product when unified purchasing program exists. Alternate supplier approval available for non-core products (90-180 days).
Must maintain highest available health/sanitary classification. Must comply with Company's higher standards beyond governmental requirements.
No video games, vending machines, coin-operated devices, lottery/gambling, alcohol, controlled substances. Smoke-free with posted signs.
Monthly electronic reports within 10 days of month end: gross receipts, sales tax amounts, other required information. Business records must be maintained and available for inspection.
Company may audit on-site or off-site at any time. If understatement ≥3%: franchisee pays all audit costs plus additional audit costs for 2 years. Sales reconstruction allowed if records inadequate.

All transfers require consent. Transfer fee $5,500 (agreement says $5,500, increases $500 on Jan 1, 2025 and each 5-year anniversary — FDD says $6,000 effective at issuance). Transferee must sign then-current agreement. All owners sign guarantee. General release required. Right of first refusal. Void if transferred without consent.

24-hour cure: goodwill/health/safety. 10-day cure: reports/payments. 30-day cure: all other. Immediate termination: loss of premises, failure to reopen, abandonment, insolvency, conviction, intentional underreporting, 3rd default in 12 months, OFAC designation.

Three formulas: (1) 2× last 12 months CLF; (2) if <12 months operating: 24 × average monthly CLF; (3) if <24 months remaining: remaining months × average monthly CLF for last 12 months. Payable within 30 days. Not applicable if location never opened.
For Company's material breach after 30-day written notice to cure. Termination effective 60 days after notice.

Stop using trademarks. Remain liable for lease and other obligations. Pay all amounts within 10 days. Company purchase option on restaurant assets at appraised value (30 days to exercise, 45 days to decide after appraisal). May deduct termination fee from purchase price.
No direct or indirect involvement in Competitive Business (QSR with hamburgers/no alcohol, or >10% revenue from ice cream/yogurt/frozen custard/fruit beverages/soft-serve/frozen treats).
1 year after expiration/termination. 500 meters of Authorized Location (Street) or same building/venue (Captive-venue). Applies to Licensee and Principal Owners.

ALL owners must sign. Joint and several guarantee of FULL performance. Personally bound by every term as if they signed the agreement individually. Waiver of demand, protest, exhaustion of remedies. Company may proceed against guarantors without action against licensee. Guarantors pay all enforcement costs. Not diminished by insolvency/bankruptcy of licensee.
Licensee indemnifies and holds harmless Company from all claims arising from Restaurant operations. Cross-referenced in Item 9.
Must maintain liability insurance at minimum designated by Company. Plus any other insurance required by law or agreements. Must furnish certificates to Company.

Arbitration in Minneapolis, MN or mutually agreed place. Injunctive relief available without exhausting arbitration for trademark matters. Prevailing party entitled to attorneys' fees. Jury trial waiver.
Federal District Court for District of Minnesota or Hennepin County District Court, Minneapolis, MN.
Law of state where Authorized Location is situated. Company may exercise rights based on reasonable business judgment.
Neither party liable for delay or failure caused by acts of God, strikes, embargoes, government orders, or other causes beyond reasonable control.
Both parties waive right to claim or recover exemplary or punitive damages. State overrides: ND — may not be enforceable.

Claims barred by earlier of: applicable statute of limitations, 1 year from discovery, or 2 years from first act/omission. Exception: underreporting, indemnification, and trademark claims subject only to applicable statutes.
Company may profit on sale of goods/services to licensee. Company may receive and retain consideration from suppliers. Consideration may or may not be related to services performed.
